title: Baby Sylus
summary: CIRI shareholder Linda Baxter of Palmer announces the birth of her grandson, Sylus Baxter, born on May 19. He was 8 pounds, 7 ounces and 22 inches long. He is the son of David Baxter, a CIRI descendant, and his wife Christina. The post Baby Sylus first appeared on CIRI .

title: Judo team member
summary: CIRI descendant Joshua Brown is training at the Olympic Training Center in Colorado Springs, Co. in Judo, a martial art. title: Honor student
summary: CIRI descendant Ray John Welcome earned a spot on the first quarter honor roll at Begich Middle School. Ray John is the son of CIRI shareholders Debbie Welcome and her husband Charles Welcome of Anchorage. title: Do you want a great summer job?
summary: Join our team and discover fun jobs, explore more of Alaska, take on leadership roles, meet new people, build your resume and try something new. title: Upcoming holiday bazaar features authentic Alaska Native-made gifts
summary: The Holiday Bazaar on Dec. 4 at the Alaska Native Heritage Center will offer gift-seekers more than 65 tables to explore with arts and crafts available for purchase from artists representing cultural groups from throughout Alaska. title: CIHA taking housing applications
summary: Cook Inlet Housing Authority is currently accepting applications for family and elder housing vacancies in Anchorage. Centennial Village in east Anchorage offers independent living for elders in beautiful, affordable one- and two-bedroom apartments located in six unique buildings. The location offer...

title: CITC’s Christmas Basket Program brings joy to families in need
summary: This holiday season, some families in our community will not have enough money to purchase gifts for their children or to celebrate with a special holiday dinner. Help spread the holiday cheer to a family in need by making a contribution to Cook Inlet Tribal Council’s annual Christmas Basket program...

title: Hamilton wins November Stock Will Prize
summary: The winner for the November 2010 Stock Will participation prize is Sunni Dawn Hamilton. summary: If you happen to visit CIRI’s executive offices, you may find Dave Swenson sitting behind the reception desk. title: CIRI Shareholder of the Year:  Mary Lou Bottorff
summary: CIRI selected Mary Lou Bottorff as its 2010 Shareholder of the Year. title: Shareholder wins Governor’s Award for Arts and Humanities
summary: CIRI shareholder and accomplished artist Lalla Williams (Sugpiaq) received a 2010 Governor’s Award for the Arts and Humanities on Oct. 21. title: Alaska Interstate Construction LLC (AIC), a CIRI company, completed construction
summary: Alaska Interstate Construction LLC (AIC), a CIRI company, completed construction of the Tanana River Bridge late summer 2010. title: CIRI to pay fourth quarter dividend, Elders’ payment
summary: On Thursday, Dec. 9, CIRI will mail or direct deposit a fourth quarter 2010 dividend of $9.83 per share. Shareholders who own 100 shares of stock will receive a dividend of $983.00. A $450 payment from CIRI’s Irrevocable Elders’ Settlement Trust will be mailed on Friday, Dec. 10, to original CIRI sh...

title: A word from the president:  Reform 8(a) rules to save and improve the program
summary: By: Margie Brown, CIRI president and CEO There has been a lot of discussion in the news and in Washington, D.C. lately about Alaska Native corporation 8(a) contracting and whether or not the program is working, should it be reformed or just thrown out. The tone and volume of the talk picked up this month when Sen. Claire McCaskill (D-Mo.) and Rep. Bennie Thompson (D-Miss.) introduced companion bills in the U.S. Senate and House to strip away contracting ad...

title: North Wind Inc. remediates groundwater at former missile site
summary: North Wind Inc., a CIRI company, is currently working under contract with the U.S. Army Corp of Engineers (USACE) to clean up contaminated soil and groundwater at a former nuclear missile site outside of Windsor, Colo. Groundwater at the site was identified as contaminated with trichloroethene (TCE)...

title: CIRI, CITC receive U.S. Census award
summary: CIRI and Cook Inlet Tribal Council (CITC) were selected to receive a 2010 United States Census Award by the Seattle Regional Census Center. The award honors individuals and organizations that show exemplary commitment to helping to relay the importance of being counted in the 2010 Census. The U.S. C...

title: Fire Island Wind works to bring renewable power to Railbelt
summary: CIRI subsidiary Fire Island Wind LLC is working hard to bring renewable power to Alaska Railbelt ratepayers at a cost that is competitive with and may soon beat natural gas-produced energy.
